_comment: Drives the /poker-near/<slug>/ city pages. Distances/drive-times are approximate from 120 FM 2821 Rd E, Huntsville TX. Repositioned around 'Pro dealers, real card room, no gimmicks.' Use only real, verifiable geography — no invented businesses, people, events, or stats.

cities.name: Huntsville
cities.route: You're already here — we're just off FM 2821, minutes from downtown and Sam Houston State.
cities.hook_1: Poker in Huntsville no longer means a long haul down I-45 to a Houston card room. Deuces Wild Poker Club is right here in town — a legal private poker club just off FM 2821, minutes from Sam Houston State University and the downtown square. Pro dealers, real card room, no gimmicks.
cities.hook_2: Four tables, forty seats, and a smaller, friendlier field than the big-city rooms. Walk in any night the doors are open — no membership needed to come check it out.
cities.intro:
For years, serious poker in Huntsville meant pointing the car down I-45 toward Houston and giving up most of an evening to the drive. That's over. Deuces Wild Poker Club is a legal private poker club operating under Texas Penal Code §47.02, tucked just off FM 2821 a few minutes from the downtown square and the Sam Houston State University campus. It's a real card room — four tables and forty seats — with professional dealers running every hand. Texas Hold'em and Omaha cash games run every night the doors are open, with Texas Hold'em tournaments Wednesday at 7 PM and Saturday at 6 PM.

The club is locally owned by Mike and Ike, who you'll usually find on-site most nights — not a back-room game and not a corporate floor, just a small, well-run room you can ask questions in. You don't need a membership to come see it for the first time, and you don't need a reservation. Park on site, walk in any night we're open, and find a seat at a real table.
cities.why_drive: From Huntsville it's barely a drive at all — five to ten minutes across town. The reason to come is the room itself: a real card room with four tables and forty seats, professional dealers at every game, and a smaller, friendlier field than the big-city options. It's a legal private club under Texas Penal Code §47.02, locally owned by Mike and Ike (usually on-site most nights), so you can meet the people running the place at the door. Cash games every night we're open, Hold'em tournaments Wednesday 7 PM and Saturday 6 PM, and walk-ins are always welcome.

faqs.q: Where exactly is Deuces Wild in Huntsville?
faqs.a: We're at 120 FM 2821 Rd E in Huntsville, just off FM 2821 and only a few minutes from the downtown square and Sam Houston State University. There's free on-site parking.

faqs.q: Is Deuces Wild a real card room or a home game?
faqs.a: It's a real card room. Deuces Wild is a legal private club operating under Texas Penal Code §47.02, with four tables and forty seats, professional dealers, and a secure cash cage on premises — not a back-room game and not a kitchen-table game.

faqs.q: Do I need a reservation or membership to play?
faqs.a: No. Walk in any night we're open — Wednesday through Saturday — and grab a seat. No reservation and no membership are needed to come check it out.

cities.hook_2: Cash games nightly, Hold'em tournaments Wednesday 7 PM and Saturday 6 PM, and a friendly room full of folks from around the lake. Walk-ins always welcome.
cities.intro:
Point Blank sits along US-190 on the Lake Livingston side of the county, which puts Deuces Wild about forty minutes west toward Huntsville. It's a short, scenic run along the lake corridor, and at the end of it is a real card room — Deuces Wild is a legal private poker club operating under Texas Penal Code §47.02, with four tables, forty seats, and professional dealers at every game.

The club is locally owned by Mike and Ike, who are usually on-site most nights. Texas Hold'em and Omaha cash games run every night the doors are open, with Texas Hold'em tournaments Wednesday at 7 PM and Saturday at 6 PM. A good part of the room comes from around the lake, so the crowd feels familiar fast. Walk in any night we're open — no reservation and no membership needed to take a seat.
cities.why_drive: Forty minutes west along the Lake Livingston corridor is a pleasant drive from Point Blank, and what makes it pay is the room: a real card room with four tables, forty seats, and professional dealers, in a legal private club under §47.02 locally owned by Mike and Ike. For a player on this side of the lake, that's the nearest serious poker — owners at the door, pros at the tables.

faqs.q: How far is Deuces Wild from Point Blank?
faqs.a: About 30 miles and roughly 40 minutes — a drive west on US-190 from Point Blank toward Huntsville.

faqs.q: Is Deuces Wild a real card room or a home game?
faqs.a: It's a real card room. Deuces Wild operates as a legal private club under Texas Penal Code §47.02, with four tables, forty seats, professional dealers, and a secure cash cage on premises.

faqs.q: Do I need a reservation coming from Point Blank?
faqs.a: No — walk in any night we're open, Wednesday through Saturday. No reservation or membership is required to play.
